SM ES2

Juliette Limozin

2023-11-10

R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com.

When you click the Knit button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

?hills
pairs(hills)

Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

Note that the echo = FALSE parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.

hills[hills$time > 50 & hills$dist <5,]
##            dist climb  time
## Knock Hill    3   350 78.65
hills[hills$time > 180 & hills$dist > 15,]
##              dist climb    time
## Bens of Jura   16  7500 204.617
## Lairig Ghru    28  2100 192.667
hills['Knock Hill', 'time'] <- hills['Knock Hill', 'time'] - 60
plot(log(hills$time) ~ log(hills$dist))

plot(log(hills$time) ~ log(hills$climb))

lm1 <- lm(log(time) ~ log(dist), data = hills)
summary(lm1)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = log(time) ~ log(dist), data = hills)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.51545 -0.12870 -0.01499  0.14193  0.48876 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  1.64237    0.12015   13.67 3.83e-15 ***
## log(dist)    1.15055    0.06255   18.39  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.2155 on 33 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.9111, Adjusted R-squared:  0.9084 
## F-statistic: 338.3 on 1 and 33 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
plot(lm1)

lm2 <- lm(log(time) ~ log(dist) + log(climb), data = hills)
summary(lm2)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = log(time) ~ log(dist) + log(climb), data = hills)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -0.52624 -0.06273  0.00452  0.06846  0.31384 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)  0.29359    0.27312   1.075     0.29    
## log(dist)    0.91141    0.06534  13.949 3.76e-15 ***
## log(climb)   0.24889    0.04761   5.228 1.02e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.1607 on 32 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.9521, Adjusted R-squared:  0.9491 
## F-statistic: 317.8 on 2 and 32 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
plot(lm2)

anova(lm1, lm2)
## Analysis of Variance Table
## 
## Model 1: log(time) ~ log(dist)
## Model 2: log(time) ~ log(dist) + log(climb)
##   Res.Df     RSS Df Sum of Sq     F    Pr(>F)    
## 1     33 1.53273                                 
## 2     32 0.82668  1   0.70605 27.33 1.024e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
predict.lm(lm2, data.frame(dist = 5.3,climb = 1100), interval = 'prediction', level = 0.95)
##        fit      lwr      upr
## 1 3.556545 3.224142 3.888948
predict.lm(lm1, data.frame(dist = 5.3,climb = 1100), interval = 'prediction', level = 0.95)
##        fit     lwr      upr
## 1 3.561158 3.11599 4.006326
paste0('Model with climb: ',exp(3.556545),' (', exp(3.224142), ', ',exp(3.888948), ')' )
## [1] "Model with climb: 35.041917929458 (25.1320016451232, 48.8594593265575)"
paste0('Model without climb: ',exp(3.561158),' (', exp(3.11599), ', ',exp(4.006326), ')' )
## [1] "Model without climb: 35.2039397127945 (22.5557494956971, 54.9446326994583)"
